question: Under what historical name is the Zwin recorded in the oldest documents, and in which 8th-century law does it form the southern border of Friezenland (Frisia)?
Which Ghent abbey held the right of patronage (patronaatsrecht) over the Mariakerk in Cadzand, and what did that right concretely entail?
In what year were polders on Cadzand first written about, and which knight then ceded his fief in favour of the St-Baafs abbey?
When were the southern and northern aisles of the Mariakerk built, in what style, and with what striking deviation?
What was the name of the original tower of the Mariakerk, and in which sea-atlas of which year does Joris Carolus describe it as a beacon?
In what year was the Peace of Cadzand concluded, and which sovereign had had the island taken the year before?
Who was the first dedicated minister (predikant) of Cadzand, in what year did he take up his post, and in what year before that did the States-General decide on a weekly preaching turn from Sluis?
Until what year did the northern aisle remain in ruins, and who donated a stained-glass window during its restoration?
expected_points: Het Vrije van Sluis (Free Region of Sluis)

question: How many hectares of land did the church of Cadzand own in 1610 compared with 1665, and what happened in 1677 to both the lands and the tower?
By whom and in which city was the current bell of Cadzand cast, and in what year?
In which three waves (years and regions of origin) did the Huguenot / Walloon refugees come to Cadzand?
expected_points: 1685 region of Calais (after revocation of the Edict of Nantes)

question: In what year did the Walloon-Reformed Congregation merge into the Reformed Congregation of Cadzand, and what physical object in the church still commemorates it today?
expected_points: chandelier in the middle of the church

question: Which treaty of which year ceded Staats-Vlaanderen to the French Republic, and in what year did the area receive the name Zeeuws-Vlaanderen?
Which king granted a subsidy in 1821 for the new roof of the church, and how many guilders did that subsidy amount to?
Which state architect (rijksbouwmeester) led the restoration from 1929 onward, and what replaced the old roof turret as the tower solution against the church?
expected_points: Renaissance-style turret (built by the civil municipality)
